title: Yosys is unable to prove logical equivalence when _DFFE_PP_ cells are duplicated
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.56+15 (git sha1 c9558b3d4, clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps


I am using `techmap -map ff_map_1.v` and `techmap -map ff_map_2.v` to replace each `_DFFE_PP_` cell with two `_DFFE_PP_` cells in the `gcd` design. I am performing logical equivalence checking by comparing the pre-retiming design with the post-retiming design. However, logical equivalence checking fails if I duplicate `_DFFE_PP_` cells.

Basically, logical equivalence checking succeeds if I don't duplicate `_DFFE_PP_` cells, and it fails when I do duplicate `_DFFE_PP_` cells.

To reproduce, run the following:
```
# Download the gcd design
git clone https://github.com/The-OpenROAD-Project/OpenROAD-flow-scripts.git
yosys synth.tcl
```

Here is `synth.tcl`
```
# Inspired by https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/302#issuecomment-276019637
proc check_logical_equivalence {top_module gold gate} {
    design -copy-from $gold -as gold $top_module
    design -copy-from $gate -as gate $top_module
    
    equiv_make -inames gold gate equiv
    prep -flatten -top equiv
    
    equiv_simple -seq 1
    equiv_induct -seq 4
    equiv_status -assert
}

yosys -import

read_verilog OpenROAD-flow-scripts/flow/designs/src/gcd/gcd.v
synth -top gcd

flatten
opt -full
clean -purge
techmap

techmap -map ff_map_1.v
techmap -map ff_map_2.v

design -save pre_retiming

abc -dff -markgroups -script "+strash; zero; &get -n; print_latch; &fraig -x; &put; scorr; dc2; retime -M 5 -D 1 -o -v; strash; &get -n; &dch -f; &nf -D 1; &put"

design -save post_retiming

check_logical_equivalence gcd pre_retiming post_retiming
```

`ff_map_1.v`:

```
module \$_DFFE_PP_ (input D, C, E, output Q);
    wire connector;
    _DFFE_PP_ _TECHMAP_REPLACE_.custom_FF_replace_1 (.D(D), .C(C), .E(E), .Q(connector));
    _DFFE_PP_ _TECHMAP_REPLACE_.custom_FF_replace_2 (.D(connector), .C(C), .E(E), .Q(Q));
endmodule
```

And `ff_map_2.v`:

```
module _DFFE_PP_ (input D, C, E, output Q);
    \$_DFFE_PP_ _TECHMAP_REPLACE_ (.D(D), .C(C), .E(E), .Q(Q));
endmodule
```

Notice that `equiv_status -assert` throws an error: "Found 110 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'."

However, comment out `techmap -map ff_map_1.v` and `techmap -map ff_map_2.v` in `synth.tcl` and run `yosys synth.tcl` again. This time, `equiv_status -assert` does not throw an error; it says "Equivalence successfully proven".

### Expected Behavior

I would expect that Yosys is able to find that two circuits are logically the same before and after retiming, even if the two circuits have duplicate `_DFFE_PP_` cells.

Is Yosys not able to find that two circuits are logically the same if they have too many DFFs? Does `abc` change the circuit too much when it retimes, causing Yosys to get confused when it runs logical equivalence checking? Please feel free to let me know if I am using Yosys incorrectly.

### Actual Behavior

Yosys is not able to find that two circuits are logically equivalent before and after retiming if the two circuits have duplicate `_DFFE_PP_` cells.
code_blocks:
# Inspired by https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/302#issuecomment-276019637
proc check_logical_equivalence {top_module gold gate} {
    design -copy-from $gold -as gold $top_module
    design -copy-from $gate -as gate $top_module
    
    equiv_make -inames gold gate equiv
    prep -flatten -top equiv
    
    equiv_simple -seq 1
    equiv_induct -seq 4
    equiv_status -assert
}

yosys -import

read_verilog OpenROAD-flow-scripts/flow/designs/src/gcd/gcd.v
synth -top gcd

flatten
opt -full
clean -purge
techmap

techmap -map ff_map_1.v
techmap -map ff_map_2.v

design -save pre_retiming

abc -dff -markgroups -script "+strash; zero; &get -n; print_latch; &fraig -x; &put; scorr; dc2; retime -M 5 -D 1 -o -v; strash; &get -n; &dch -f; &nf -D 1; &put"

design -save post_retiming

check_logical_equivalence gcd pre_retiming post_retiming
